'' : '') 23→ inList = false 24→ listType = null 25→ } 26→ } 27→ 28→ while (i < lines.length) { 29→ const line = lines[i] ?? '' 30→ 31→ // Fenced code block 32→ if (line.startsWith('```')) { 33→ closeList() 34→ const lang = line.slice(3).trim() 35→ const codeLines: string[] = [] 36→ i++ 37→ while (i < lines.length && !(lines[i] ?? '').startsWith('```')) { 38→ codeLines.push(lines[i] ?? '') 39→ i++ 40→ } 41→ output.push(`
${escHtml(codeLines.join('\n'))}
`) 42→ i++ 43→ continue 44→ } 45→ 46→ // YAML front matter (skip) 47→ if (i === 0 && line === '---') { 48→ i++ 49→ while (i < lines.length && (lines[i] ?? '') !== '---') i++ 50→ i++ // skip closing --- 51→ continue 52→ } 53→ 54→ // Headings 55→ const headingMatch = line.match(/^(#{1,6})\s+(.*)$/) 56→ if (headingMatch) { 57→ closeList() 58→ const level = headingMatch[1]?.length ?? 1 59→ const text = headingMatch[2] ?? '' 60→ output.push(`${applyInline(text)}`) 61→ i++ 62→ continue 63→ } 64→ 65→ // Horizontal rule 66→ if (/^---+$/.test(line.trim())) { 67→ closeList() 68→ output.push('
') 69→ i++ 70→ continue 71→ } 72→ 73→ // Ordered list 74→ const olMatch = line.match(/^(\s*)\d+\.\s+(.+)$/) 75→ if (olMatch) { 76→ if (!inList || listType !== 'ol') { 77→ closeList() 78→ output.push('
    ') 79→ inList = true 80→ listType = 'ol' 81→ } 82→ output.push(`
  1. ${applyInline(olMatch[2] ?? '')}
    2. `) 83→ i++ 84→ continue 85→ } 86→ 87→ // Unordered list 88→ const ulMatch = line.match(/^(\s*)[-*+]\s+(.+)$/) 89→ if (ulMatch) { 90→ if (!inList || listType !== 'ul') { 91→ closeList() 92→ output.push('
      ') 93→ inList = true 94→ listType = 'ul' 95→ } 96→ output.push(`
    • ${applyInline(ulMatch[2] ?? '')}

'' : '') 23→ inList = false 24→ listType = null 25→ } 26→ } 27→ 28→ while (i < lines.length) { 29→ const line = lines[i] ?? '' 30→ 31→ // Fenced code block 32→ if (line.startsWith('```')) { 33→ closeList() 34→ const lang = line.slice(3).trim() 35→ const codeLines: string[] = [] 36→ i++ 37→ while (i < lines.length && !(lines[i] ?? '').startsWith('```')) { 38→ codeLines.push(lines[i] ?? '') 39→ i++ 40→ } 41→ output.push(`
${escHtml(codeLines.join('\n'))}
`) 42→ i++ 43→ continue 44→ } 45→ 46→ // YAML front matter (skip) 47→ if (i === 0 && line === '---') { 48→ i++ 49→ while (i < lines.length && (lines[i] ?? '') !== '---') i++ 50→ i++ // skip closing --- 51→ continue 52→ } 53→ 54→ // Headings 55→ const headingMatch = line.match(/^(#{1,6})\s+(.*)$/) 56→ if (headingMatch) { 57→ closeList() 58→ const level = headingMatch[1]?.length ?? 1 59→ const text = headingMatch[2] ?? '' 60→ output.push(`${applyInline(text)}`) 61→ i++ 62→ continue 63→ } 64→ 65→ // Horizontal rule 66→ if (/^---+$/.test(line.trim())) { 67→ closeList() 68→ output.push('
') 69→ i++ 70→ continue 71→ } 72→ 73→ // Ordered list 74→ const olMatch = line.match(/^(\s*)\d+\.\s+(.+)$/) 75→ if (olMatch) { 76→ if (!inList || listType !== 'ol') { 77→ closeList() 78→ output.push('
    ') 79→ inList = true 80→ listType = 'ol' 81→ } 82→ output.push(`
  1. ${applyInline(olMatch[2] ?? '')}
    2. `) 83→ i++ 84→ continue 85→ } 86→ 87→ // Unordered list 88→ const ulMatch = line.match(/^(\s*)[-*+]\s+(.+)$/) 89→ if (ulMatch) { 90→ if (!inList || listType !== 'ul') { 91→ closeList() 92→ output.push('

1→import { describe, it, expect } from 'vitest'
     2→import type { ManifestEntry } from '../../src/types/transcript.js'
     3→import {
     4→  computeStats,
     5→  buildDayBuckets,
     6→  buildWeekBuckets,

    10→
    11→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    12→// Helpers
    13→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    14→
    15→function makeManifest(overrides: Partial<ManifestEntry> = {}): ManifestEntry {
    16→  return {
    17→    client: 'codex',
    18→    project: 'test',
    19→    session_id: 'abc-123',
    20→    raw_sha256: 'deadbeef',
    21→    title: 'Test Session',
    22→    started_at: '2026-03-10T10:00:00Z',
    23→    imported_at: '2026-03-10T10:01:00Z',
    24→    cwd: '/home/user',
    25→    branch: 'main',
    26→    raw_source_path: '/src/file.jsonl',
    27→    raw_upload_permission: 'not-set',

    29→    global_clean_path: '',

    31→    repo_raw_path: '',
    32→    repo_clean_path: '',
    33→    repo_manifest_path: '',
    34→    message_count: 10,
    35→    tool_event_count: 5,
    36→    cleaning_mode: 'deterministic-code',
    37→    repo_mirror_enabled: true,
    38→    ...overrides,
    39→  }
    40→}
    41→

    44→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    45→
    46→describe('computeStats', () => {
    47→  it('handles zero manifests', () => {
    48→    const stats = computeStats([])
    49→    expect(stats.totalSessions).toBe(0)
    50→    expect(stats.totalMessages).toBe(0)
    51→    expect(stats.totalToolEvents).toBe(0)
    52→    expect(stats.activeDays).toBe(0)
    53→    expect(stats.dateRange.from).toBe('')
    54→    expect(stats.dateRange.to).toBe('')
    55→    expect(stats.clientCounts).toEqual({})
    56→    expect(stats.dayBuckets).toEqual({})
    57→    expect(stats.weekBuckets).toEqual({})
    58→    expect(stats.toolCounts).toEqual({})
    59→  })
    60→
    61→  it('sums messages and tool events', () => {
    62→    const m1 = makeManifest({ message_count: 10, tool_event_count: 5 })
    63→    const m2 = makeManifest({ session_id: 'xyz', message_count: 20, tool_event_count: 3 })
    64→    const stats = computeStats([m1, m2])
    65→    expect(stats.totalSessions).toBe(2)
    66→    expect(stats.totalMessages).toBe(30)
    67→    expect(stats.totalToolEvents).toBe(8)
    68→  })
    69→
    70→  it('counts client distribution', () => {
    71→    const m1 = makeManifest({ client: 'codex' })
    72→    const m2 = makeManifest({ session_id: 'b', client: 'claude' })
    73→    const m3 = makeManifest({ session_id: 'c', client: 'claude' })
    74→    const stats = computeStats([m1, m2, m3])
    75→    expect(stats.clientCounts['codex']).toBe(1)
    76→    expect(stats.clientCounts['claude']).toBe(2)
    77→  })
    78→
    79→  it('counts active days correctly', () => {
    80→    const m1 = makeManifest({ started_at: '2026-03-10T10:00:00Z' })
    81→    const m2 = makeManifest({ session_id: 'b', started_at: '2026-03-10T14:00:00Z' }) // same day
    82→    const m3 = makeManifest({ session_id: 'c', started_at: '2026-03-11T08:00:00Z' }) // different day
    83→    const stats = computeStats([m1, m2, m3])
    84→    expect(stats.activeDays).toBe(2)
    85→  })
    86→
    87→  it('computes date range', () => {
    88→    const m1 = makeManifest({ started_at: '2026-01-01T00:00:00Z' })
    89→    const m2 = makeManifest({ session_id: 'b', started_at: '2026-03-15T00:00:00Z' })

    93→  })
    94→
    95→  it('accepts external toolCounts', () => {
    96→    const stats = computeStats([], { Bash: 10, Read: 5 })
    97→    expect(stats.toolCounts['Bash']).toBe(10)
    98→    expect(stats.toolCounts['Read']).toBe(5)
    99→  })
   100→})

   112→    const m1 = makeManifest({ started_at: '2026-03-10T10:00:00Z' })
   113→    const m2 = makeManifest({ session_id: 'b', started_at: '2026-03-10T14:00:00Z' })
   114→    const m3 = makeManifest({ session_id: 'c', started_at: '2026-03-11T08:00:00Z' })
   115→    const buckets = buildDayBuckets([m1, m2, m3])
   116→    expect(buckets['2026-03-10']).toBe(2)
   117→    expect(buckets['2026-03-11']).toBe(1)
   118→  })
   119→
   120→  it('ignores manifests with empty started_at', () => {
   121→    const m = makeManifest({ started_at: '' })
   122→    expect(buildDayBuckets([m])).toEqual({})
   123→  })
   124→})
   125→
   126→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

   128→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   129→
   130→describe('buildWeekBuckets', () => {

   133→  })
   134→
   135→  it('buckets messages by ISO week', () => {
   136→    // 2026-03-10 is in week 2026-W11
   137→    const m1 = makeManifest({ started_at: '2026-03-10T10:00:00Z', message_count: 5 })
   138→    const m2 = makeManifest({ session_id: 'b', started_at: '2026-03-11T10:00:00Z', message_count: 3 })
   139→    const buckets = buildWeekBuckets([m1, m2])
   140→    // Both are in the same week
   141→    const weekKey = Object.keys(buckets)[0]
   142→    expect(weekKey).toMatch(/^\d{4}-W\d{2}$/)
   143→    const val = weekKey ? buckets[weekKey] : 0
   144→    expect(val).toBe(8)
   145→  })
   146→})
   147→
   148→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   149→// extractToolNames
   150→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   151→
   152→describe('extractToolNames', () => {

   155→    expect(names[0]).toBe('Bash')
   156→    expect(names[1]).toBe('Read')
   157→  })
   158→
   159→  it('handles underscore and hyphen in names', () => {
   160→    const names = extractToolNames(['function_call input=...', 'custom-tool arg=1'])
   161→    expect(names[0]).toBe('function_call')
   162→    expect(names[1]).toBe('custom-tool')
   163→  })
   164→
   165→  it('returns unknown for non-identifier start', () => {
   166→    const names = extractToolNames(['123abc', '!cmd'])
   167→    expect(names[0]).toBe('unknown')
   168→    expect(names[1]).toBe('unknown')
   169→  })
   170→
   171→  it('handles empty array', () => {
   172→    expect(extractToolNames([])).toEqual([])
   173→  })
   174→
   175→  it('handles empty string entries', () => {
   176→    const names = extractToolNames([''])
   177→    expect(names[0]).toBe('unknown')
   178→  })
   179→})
   180→
   181→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   182→// accumulateToolCounts
   183→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   184→
   185→describe('accumulateToolCounts', () => {
   186→  it('accumulates counts into new map', () => {

   189→    expect(result['Bash']).toBe(5)
   190→    expect(result['Read']).toBe(1)
   191→  })
   192→

   195→    accumulateToolCounts(base, ['Bash'])
   196→    expect(base['Bash']).toBe(1)
   197→  })
   198→})

1→import { describe, it, expect, beforeEach, afterEach, vi } from 'vitest'
     2→import { mkdtempSync, rmSync, existsSync } from 'node:fs'
     3→import { join } from 'node:path'
     4→import { tmpdir } from 'node:os'
     5→import {
     6→  buildSummaryPrompt,
     7→  readCache,
     8→  writeCache,
     9→  getSummary,
    10→} from '../../src/report/summarize.js'
    11→import type { SummaryMessage } from '../../src/report/summarize.js'
    12→
    13→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    14→// Setup
    15→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    16→
    17→let tmpDir: string
    18→
    19→beforeEach(() => {
    20→  tmpDir = mkdtempSync(join(tmpdir(), 'summarize-test-'))
    21→  // Ensure ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is not set for most tests
    22→  delete process.env['ANTHROPIC_API_KEY']
    23→})
    24→
    25→afterEach(() => {
    26→  rmSync(tmpDir, { recursive: true, force: true })
    27→  delete process.env['ANTHROPIC_API_KEY']
    28→  vi.restoreAllMocks()
    29→})
    30→
    31→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    32→// buildSummaryPrompt
    33→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    34→
    35→describe('buildSummaryPrompt', () => {
    36→  it('includes role and text for each message', () => {
    37→    const msgs: SummaryMessage[] = [
    38→      { role: 'user', text: 'Hello world' },
    39→      { role: 'assistant', text: 'Hi there' },
    40→    ]
    41→    const prompt = buildSummaryPrompt(msgs)
    42→    expect(prompt).toContain('USER: Hello world')
    43→    expect(prompt).toContain('ASSISTANT: Hi there')
    44→  })
    45→
    46→  it('truncates message text to 500 chars', () => {
    47→    const longText = 'x'.repeat(600)
    48→    const msgs: SummaryMessage[] = [{ role: 'user', text: longText }]
    49→    const prompt = buildSummaryPrompt(msgs)
    50→    const line = prompt.split('\n')[0] ?? ''
    51→    // "USER: " + 500 chars = 506 chars max
    52→    expect(line.length).toBeLessThanOrEqual(506)
    53→  })
    54→
    55→  it('limits to first 30 messages', () => {
    56→    const msgs: SummaryMessage[] = Array.from({ length: 50 }, (_, i) => ({
    57→      role: i % 2 === 0 ? 'user' : 'assistant',
    58→      text: `Message ${i}`,
    59→    }))
    60→    const prompt = buildSummaryPrompt(msgs)
    61→    // Prompt should not contain message 30+
    62→    expect(prompt).not.toContain('Message 30')
    63→    expect(prompt).toContain('Message 29')
    64→  })
    65→
    66→  it('handles empty messages array', () => {
    67→    const prompt = buildSummaryPrompt([])
    68→    expect(prompt).toBe('')
    69→  })
    70→})
    71→
    72→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    73→// Cache read/write
    74→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    75→
    76→describe('readCache / writeCache', () => {
    77→  it('returns null for cache miss', () => {
    78→    const result = readCache('nonexistent-sha256', tmpDir)
    79→    expect(result).toBeNull()
    80→  })
    81→
    82→  it('round-trips: write then read', () => {
    83→    writeCache('abc123', 'Great session summary.', tmpDir)
    84→    const result = readCache('abc123', tmpDir)
    85→    expect(result).toBe('Great session summary.')
    86→  })
    87→
    88→  it('creates cache file at correct path', () => {
    89→    writeCache('sha256abc', 'some summary', tmpDir)
    90→    expect(existsSync(join(tmpDir, 'sha256abc.json'))).toBe(true)
    91→  })
    92→
    93→  it('returns null for corrupted cache file', async () => {
    94→    const { writeFileSync } = await import('node:fs')
    95→    writeFileSync(join(tmpDir, 'bad.json'), 'not json!!!', 'utf-8')
    96→    expect(readCache('bad', tmpDir)).toBeNull()
    97→  })
    98→})
    99→
   100→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   101→// getSummary
   102→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   103→
   104→describe('getSummary', () => {
   105→  it('returns cached summary without API call', async () => {
   106→    writeCache('cachedhash', 'Cached result', tmpDir)
   107→    const result = await getSummary('cachedhash', [], {
   108→      cacheDir: tmpDir,
   109→      noAi: false,
   110→      model: 'claude-haiku-4-5-20251001',
   111→    })

1→import { describe, it, expect, beforeEach, afterEach, vi } from 'vitest'
     2→import { mkdtempSync, rmSync, existsSync } from 'node:fs'
     3→import { join } from 'node:path'
     4→import { tmpdir } from 'node:os'
     5→import {
     6→  buildSummaryPrompt,
     7→  readCache,
     8→  writeCache,
     9→  getSummary,
    10→} from '../../src/report/summarize.js'
    11→import type { SummaryMessage } from '../../src/report/summarize.js'
    12→
    13→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    14→// Setup
    15→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    16→
    17→let tmpDir: string
    18→
    19→beforeEach(() => {
    20→  tmpDir = mkdtempSync(join(tmpdir(), 'summarize-test-'))
    21→  // Ensure ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is not set for most tests
    22→  delete process.env['ANTHROPIC_API_KEY']
    23→})
    24→
    25→afterEach(() => {
    26→  rmSync(tmpDir, { recursive: true, force: true })
    27→  delete process.env['ANTHROPIC_API_KEY']
    28→  vi.restoreAllMocks()
    29→})
    30→
    31→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    32→// buildSummaryPrompt
    33→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    34→
    35→describe('buildSummaryPrompt', () => {
    36→  it('includes role and text for each message', () => {
    37→    const msgs: SummaryMessage[] = [
    38→      { role: 'user', text: 'Hello world' },
    39→      { role: 'assistant', text: 'Hi there' },
    40→    ]
    41→    const prompt = buildSummaryPrompt(msgs)
    42→    expect(prompt).toContain('USER: Hello world')
    43→    expect(prompt).toContain('ASSISTANT: Hi there')
    44→  })
    45→
    46→  it('truncates message text to 500 chars', () => {
    47→    const longText = 'x'.repeat(600)
    48→    const msgs: SummaryMessage[] = [{ role: 'user', text: longText }]
    49→    const prompt = buildSummaryPrompt(msgs)
    50→    const line = prompt.split('\n')[0] ?? ''
    51→    // "USER: " + 500 chars = 506 chars max
    52→    expect(line.length).toBeLessThanOrEqual(506)
    53→  })
    54→
    55→  it('limits to first 30 messages', () => {
    56→    const msgs: SummaryMessage[] = Array.from({ length: 50 }, (_, i) => ({
    57→      role: i % 2 === 0 ? 'user' : 'assistant',
    58→      text: `Message ${i}`,
    59→    }))
    60→    const prompt = buildSummaryPrompt(msgs)
    61→    // Prompt should not contain message 30+
    62→    expect(prompt).not.toContain('Message 30')
    63→    expect(prompt).toContain('Message 29')
    64→  })
    65→
    66→  it('handles empty messages array', () => {
    67→    const prompt = buildSummaryPrompt([])
    68→    expect(prompt).toBe('')
    69→  })
    70→})
    71→
    72→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    73→// Cache read/write
    74→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    75→
    76→describe('readCache / writeCache', () => {
    77→  it('returns null for cache miss', () => {
    78→    const result = readCache('nonexistent-sha256', tmpDir)
    79→    expect(result).toBeNull()
    80→  })
    81→
    82→  it('round-trips: write then read', () => {
    83→    writeCache('abc123', 'Great session summary.', tmpDir)
    84→    const result = readCache('abc123', tmpDir)
    85→    expect(result).toBe('Great session summary.')
    86→  })
    87→
    88→  it('creates cache file at correct path', () => {
    89→    writeCache('sha256abc', 'some summary', tmpDir)
    90→    expect(existsSync(join(tmpDir, 'sha256abc.json'))).toBe(true)
    91→  })
    92→
    93→  it('returns null for corrupted cache file', async () => {
    94→    const { writeFileSync } = await import('node:fs')
    95→    writeFileSync(join(tmpDir, 'bad.json'), 'not json!!!', 'utf-8')
    96→    expect(readCache('bad', tmpDir)).toBeNull()
    97→  })
    98→})
    99→
   100→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   101→// getSummary
   102→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   103→
   104→describe('getSummary', () => {
   105→  it('returns cached summary without API call', async () => {
   106→    writeCache('cachedhash', 'Cached result', tmpDir)
   107→    const result = await getSummary('cachedhash', [], {
   108→      cacheDir: tmpDir,
   109→      noAi: false,
   110→      model: 'claude-haiku-4-5-20251001',
   111→    })
   112→    expect(result).toBe('Cached result')
   113→  })
   114→
   115→  it('returns empty string when noAi=true', async () => {
   116→    const result = await getSummary('newsha', [], {
   117→      cacheDir: tmpDir,
   118→      noAi: true,
   119→      model: 'claude-haiku-4-5-20251001',
   120→    })
   121→    expect(result).toBe('')
   122→  })
   123→
   124→  it('returns empty string when ANTHROPIC_API_KEY not set', async () => {
   125→    delete process.env['ANTHROPIC_API_KEY']
   126→    const result = await getSummary('newsha2', [], {
   127→      cacheDir: tmpDir,
   128→      noAi: false,
   129→      model: 'claude-haiku-4-5-20251001',
   130→    })
   131→    expect(result).toBe('')
   132→  })
   133→
   134→  it('prefers cache over API even when key is set', async () => {
   135→    process.env['ANTHROPIC_API_KEY'] = 'sk-test-key'
   136→    writeCache('cachedwithkey', 'Cached!', tmpDir)
   137→    const result = await getSummary('cachedwithkey', [], {
   138→      cacheDir: tmpDir,
   139→      noAi: false,
   140→      model: 'claude-haiku-4-5-20251001',
   141→    })
   142→    expect(result).toBe('Cached!')
   143→  })
   144→})
   145→

1→import { describe, it, expect } from 'vitest'
     2→import type { ManifestEntry } from '../../../src/types/transcript.js'
     3→import { buildSearchIndex, renderSearchPage } from '../../../src/report/render/search.js'
     4→import type { SearchIndexEntry } from '../../../src/types/report.js'
     5→
     6→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
     7→// Helpers
     8→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
     9→
    10→function makeManifest(id: string, client = 'codex', repoClean = ''): ManifestEntry {
    11→  return {
    12→    client,
    13→    project: 'test',
    14→    session_id: id,
    15→    raw_sha256: id.padEnd(64, '0'),
    16→    title: `Session ${id}`,
    17→    started_at: '2026-03-10T10:00:00Z',
    18→    imported_at: '2026-03-10T10:01:00Z',
    19→    cwd: '',
    20→    branch: 'main',
    21→    raw_source_path: '',
    22→    raw_upload_permission: 'not-set',
    23→    global_raw_path: '',
    24→    global_clean_path: '',
    25→    global_manifest_path: '',
    26→    repo_raw_path: '',
    27→    repo_clean_path: repoClean,
    28→    repo_manifest_path: '',
    29→    message_count: 5,
    30→    tool_event_count: 2,
    31→    cleaning_mode: 'deterministic-code',
    32→    repo_mirror_enabled: true,
    33→  }
    34→}
    35→
    36→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    37→// buildSearchIndex
    38→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    39→
    40→describe('buildSearchIndex', () => {
    41→  it('builds index with correct fields', () => {
    42→    const m = makeManifest('abc', 'codex', 'Memory/06_transcripts/clean/codex/2026/03/abc__deadbeef.md')
    43→    const index = buildSearchIndex([m], () => 'some snippet')
    44→    expect(index).toHaveLength(1)
    45→    const entry = index[0]!
    46→    expect(entry.title).toBe('Session abc')
    47→    expect(entry.client).toBe('codex')
    48→    expect(entry.date).toBe('2026-03-10')
    49→    expect(entry.snippet).toBe('some snippet')
    50→  })
    51→
    52→  it('uses session_id as stem when no clean path', () => {
    53→    const m = makeManifest('myid', 'claude', '')
    54→    const index = buildSearchIndex([m], () => '')
    55→    expect(index[0]!.url).toContain('myid')
    56→  })
    57→
    58→  it('produces correct URL format', () => {
    59→    const m = makeManifest('xyz', 'claude', 'Memory/06_transcripts/clean/claude/2026/03/xyz__abc.md')
    60→    const index = buildSearchIndex([m], () => '')
    61→    expect(index[0]!.url).toBe('transcripts/claude/xyz__abc.html')
    62→  })
    63→
    64→  it('truncates snippets to 200 chars', () => {
    65→    const longSnippet = 'x'.repeat(300)
    66→    const m = makeManifest('a')
    67→    const index = buildSearchIndex([m], () => longSnippet)
    68→    expect(index[0]!.snippet.length).toBeLessThanOrEqual(200)
    69→  })
    70→
    71→  it('respects 50kB total index budget', () => {
    72→    const manifests: ManifestEntry[] = []
    73→    for (let i = 0; i < 100; i++) {
    74→      manifests.push(makeManifest(`session-${i}`))
    75→    }
    76→    // Give each session a large snippet
    77→    const bigSnippet = 'a'.repeat(200)
    78→    const index = buildSearchIndex(manifests, () => bigSnippet)
    79→    const json = JSON.stringify(index)
    80→    expect(json.length).toBeLessThanOrEqual(51_000) // some tolerance
    81→  })
    82→
    83→  it('returns empty array for no manifests', () => {
    84→    expect(buildSearchIndex([], () => '')).toEqual([])
    85→  })
    86→})
    87→

    90→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    91→
    92→describe('renderSearchPage', () => {
    93→  it('embeds SEARCH_INDEX as inline JS', () => {
    94→    const index: SearchIndexEntry[] = [
    95→      { url: 'transcripts/codex/a.html', title: 'Test', client: 'codex', date: '2026-03-10', snippet: 'hi' },
    96→    ]
    97→    const html = renderSearchPage(index)
    98→    expect(html).toContain('const SEARCH_INDEX =')
    99→    expect(html).toContain('"transcripts/codex/a.html"')
   100→  })
   101→
   102→  it('produces valid HTML structure', () => {
   103→    const html = renderSearchPage([])
   104→    expect(html).toContain('<!DOCTYPE html>')
   105→    expect(html).toContain('<title>')

1→import { describe, it, expect } from 'vitest'
     2→import type { ManifestEntry } from '../../../src/types/transcript.js'
     3→import { buildSearchIndex, renderSearchPage } from '../../../src/report/render/search.js'
     4→import type { SearchIndexEntry } from '../../../src/types/report.js'
     5→
     6→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
     7→// Helpers
     8→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
     9→
    10→function makeManifest(id: string, client = 'codex', repoClean = ''): ManifestEntry {
    11→  return {
    12→    client,
    13→    project: 'test',
    14→    session_id: id,
    15→    raw_sha256: id.padEnd(64, '0'),
    16→    title: `Session ${id}`,
    17→    started_at: '2026-03-10T10:00:00Z',
    18→    imported_at: '2026-03-10T10:01:00Z',
    19→    cwd: '',
    20→    branch: 'main',
    21→    raw_source_path: '',
    22→    raw_upload_permission: 'not-set',
    23→    global_raw_path: '',
    24→    global_clean_path: '',
    25→    global_manifest_path: '',
    26→    repo_raw_path: '',
    27→    repo_clean_path: repoClean,
    28→    repo_manifest_path: '',
    29→    message_count: 5,
    30→    tool_event_count: 2,
    31→    cleaning_mode: 'deterministic-code',
    32→    repo_mirror_enabled: true,
    33→  }
    34→}
    35→
    36→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    37→// buildSearchIndex
    38→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    39→
    40→describe('buildSearchIndex', () => {
    41→  it('builds index with correct fields', () => {
    42→    const m = makeManifest('abc', 'codex', 'Memory/06_transcripts/clean/codex/2026/03/abc__deadbeef.md')
    43→    const index = buildSearchIndex([m], () => 'some snippet')
    44→    expect(index).toHaveLength(1)
    45→    const entry = index[0]!
    46→    expect(entry.title).toBe('Session abc')
    47→    expect(entry.client).toBe('codex')
    48→    expect(entry.date).toBe('2026-03-10')
    49→    expect(entry.snippet).toBe('some snippet')
    50→  })
    51→
    52→  it('uses session_id as stem when no clean path', () => {
    53→    const m = makeManifest('myid', 'claude', '')
    54→    const index = buildSearchIndex([m], () => '')
    55→    expect(index[0]!.url).toContain('myid')
    56→  })
    57→
    58→  it('produces correct URL format', () => {
    59→    const m = makeManifest('xyz', 'claude', 'Memory/06_transcripts/clean/claude/2026/03/xyz__abc.md')
    60→    const index = buildSearchIndex([m], () => '')
    61→    expect(index[0]!.url).toBe('transcripts/claude/xyz__abc.html')
    62→  })
    63→
    64→  it('truncates snippets to 200 chars', () => {
    65→    const longSnippet = 'x'.repeat(300)
    66→    const m = makeManifest('a')
    67→    const index = buildSearchIndex([m], () => longSnippet)
    68→    expect(index[0]!.snippet.length).toBeLessThanOrEqual(200)
    69→  })
    70→
    71→  it('respects 50kB total index budget', () => {
    72→    const manifests: ManifestEntry[] = []
    73→    for (let i = 0; i < 100; i++) {
    74→      manifests.push(makeManifest(`session-${i}`))
    75→    }
    76→    // Give each session a large snippet
    77→    const bigSnippet = 'a'.repeat(200)
    78→    const index = buildSearchIndex(manifests, () => bigSnippet)
    79→    const json = JSON.stringify(index)
    80→    expect(json.length).toBeLessThanOrEqual(51_000) // some tolerance
    81→  })
    82→
    83→  it('returns empty array for no manifests', () => {
    84→    expect(buildSearchIndex([], () => '')).toEqual([])
    85→  })
    86→})
    87→
    88→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    89→// renderSearchPage
    90→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    91→
    92→describe('renderSearchPage', () => {
    93→  it('embeds SEARCH_INDEX as inline JS', () => {
    94→    const index: SearchIndexEntry[] = [
    95→      { url: 'transcripts/codex/a.html', title: 'Test', client: 'codex', date: '2026-03-10', snippet: 'hi' },
    96→    ]
    97→    const html = renderSearchPage(index)
    98→    expect(html).toContain('const SEARCH_INDEX =')
    99→    expect(html).toContain('"transcripts/codex/a.html"')
   100→  })
   101→
   102→  it('produces valid HTML structure', () => {
   103→    const html = renderSearchPage([])
   104→    expect(html).toContain('<!DOCTYPE html>')
   105→    expect(html).toContain('<title>')
   106→    expect(html).toContain('search-input')
   107→  })
   108→
   109→  it('embeds valid JSON in the index', () => {
   110→    const index: SearchIndexEntry[] = [
   111→      { url: 'a.html', title: 'Test <session>', client: 'codex', date: '2026-03-10', snippet: 'body' },
   112→    ]
   113→    const html = renderSearchPage(index)
   114→    // Extract the JSON part
   115→    const match = html.match(/const SEARCH_INDEX = (\[[\s\S]*?\]);/)
   116→    expect(match).not.toBeNull()
   117→    expect(() => JSON.parse(match![1]!)).not.toThrow()
   118→  })
   119→
   120→  it('includes vanilla JS search logic', () => {
   121→    const html = renderSearchPage([])
   122→    expect(html).toContain('addEventListener')
   123→    expect(html).toContain('SEARCH_INDEX')
   124→    expect(html).toContain('toLowerCase')
   125→  })
   126→})
   127→

1→import { describe, it, expect, beforeEach, afterEach } from 'vitest'
     2→import { mkdtempSync, mkdirSync, writeFileSync, rmSync } from 'node:fs'
     3→import { join } from 'node:path'
     4→import { tmpdir } from 'node:os'
     5→import type { ManifestEntry } from '../../../src/types/transcript.js'
     6→import { extractLinks, buildLinkGraph } from '../../../src/report/render/links.js'
     7→
     8→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
     9→// Helpers
    10→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    11→
    12→let tmpDir: string
    13→
    14→beforeEach(() => {
    15→  tmpDir = mkdtempSync(join(tmpdir(), 'links-test-'))
    16→})
    17→
    18→afterEach(() => {
    19→  rmSync(tmpDir, { recursive: true, force: true })
    20→})
    21→
    22→function makeManifest(id: string, stem: string, content?: string): ManifestEntry {
    23→  const cleanRelPath = `Memory/06_transcripts/clean/codex/2026/03/${stem}.md`
    24→  if (content !== undefined) {
    25→    const fullPath = join(tmpDir, cleanRelPath)
    26→    mkdirSync(join(tmpDir, 'Memory/06_transcripts/clean/codex/2026/03'), { recursive: true })
    27→    writeFileSync(fullPath, content, 'utf-8')
    28→  }
    29→  return {
    30→    client: 'codex',
    31→    project: 'test',
    32→    session_id: id,
    33→    raw_sha256: id.slice(0, 8).padEnd(64, '0'),
    34→    title: `Session ${id}`,
    35→    started_at: '2026-03-10T10:00:00Z',
    36→    imported_at: '2026-03-10T10:01:00Z',
    37→    cwd: '',
    38→    branch: 'main',
    39→    raw_source_path: '',
    40→    raw_upload_permission: 'not-set',
    41→    global_raw_path: '',
    42→    global_clean_path: '',
    43→    global_manifest_path: '',
    44→    repo_raw_path: '',
    45→    repo_clean_path: cleanRelPath,
    46→    repo_manifest_path: '',
    47→    message_count: 1,
    48→    tool_event_count: 0,
    49→    cleaning_mode: 'deterministic-code',
    50→    repo_mirror_enabled: true,
    51→  }
    52→}
    53→
    54→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    55→// extractLinks
    56→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    57→
    58→describe('extractLinks', () => {
    59→  it('detects [[id]] references', () => {
    60→    const refs = extractLinks('See [[session-abc]] for details.')
    61→    expect(refs).toContain('session-abc')
    62→  })
    63→
    64→  it('detects multiple references', () => {
    65→    const refs = extractLinks('[[a]] and [[b]] are related.')
    66→    expect(refs).toHaveLength(2)
    67→    expect(refs).toContain('a')
    68→    expect(refs).toContain('b')
    69→  })
    70→
    71→  it('ignores partial brackets: single bracket', () => {
    72→    const refs = extractLinks('[not a link] and [another')
    73→    expect(refs).toHaveLength(0)
    74→  })
    75→
    76→  it('ignores newlines inside brackets', () => {
    77→    const refs = extractLinks('[[line1\nline2]]')
    78→    expect(refs).toHaveLength(0)
    79→  })
    80→
    81→  it('trims whitespace from refs', () => {
    82→    const refs = extractLinks('[[ spaced ]]')
    83→    expect(refs[0]).toBe('spaced')
    84→  })
    85→
    86→  it('returns empty for text with no links', () => {
    87→    expect(extractLinks('No links here.')).toEqual([])
    88→  })
    89→})
    90→
    91→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    92→// buildLinkGraph
    93→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    94→
    95→describe('buildLinkGraph', () => {
    96→  it('builds forward and back links', () => {
    97→    const m1 = makeManifest('aaa', 'aaa__00000000', '## Messages\n### 1. user\nSee [[bbb]]')
    98→    const m2 = makeManifest('bbb', 'bbb__00000000', '## Messages\n### 1. user\nNo links here')
    99→
   100→    const graph = buildLinkGraph([m1, m2], tmpDir)
   101→    expect(graph.forwardLinks['aaa']).toContain('bbb')
   102→    expect(graph.backlinks['bbb']).toContain('aaa')
   103→  })
   104→
   105→  it('ignores self-references', () => {
   106→    const m1 = makeManifest('aaa', 'aaa__00000000', '## Messages\n### 1. user\nSee [[aaa]]')
   107→
   108→    const graph = buildLinkGraph([m1], tmpDir)
   109→    expect(graph.forwardLinks['aaa'] ?? []).toHaveLength(0)
   110→    expect(graph.backlinks['aaa'] ?? []).toHaveLength(0)
   111→  })
   112→
   113→  it('handles manifests with no clean file', () => {
   114→    const m = makeManifest('zzz', 'zzz__00000000') // no content written
   115→    const graph = buildLinkGraph([m], tmpDir)
   116→    expect(graph.forwardLinks).toEqual({})
   117→    expect(graph.backlinks).toEqual({})
   118→  })
   119→
   120→  it('deduplicates links', () => {
   121→    const m1 = makeManifest('aaa', 'aaa__00000000', '## Messages\n### 1. user\n[[bbb]] [[bbb]] again')
   122→    const m2 = makeManifest('bbb', 'bbb__00000000', '')
   123→
   124→    const graph = buildLinkGraph([m1, m2], tmpDir)
   125→    const fwd = graph.forwardLinks['aaa'] ?? []
   126→    const count = fwd.filter(x => x === 'bbb').length
   127→    expect(count).toBe(1)
   128→  })
   129→
   130→  it('matches by stem as well as session_id', () => {
   131→    // The link text is the stem of the clean file
   132→    const m1 = makeManifest('aaa', 'aaa__00000000', '## Messages\n### 1. user\nSee [[bbb__11111111]]')
   133→    const m2 = makeManifest('bbb', 'bbb__11111111', '')
   134→
   135→    const graph = buildLinkGraph([m1, m2], tmpDir)
   136→    expect(graph.forwardLinks['aaa']).toContain('bbb')
   137→  })
   138→})
   139→

events.output:
1→/**
     2→ * MemoryTree CLI — unified entry point.
     3→ * Registers all subcommands via commander.
     4→ */
     5→
     6→import { Command } from 'commander'
     7→
     8→const program = new Command()
     9→
    10→program
    11→  .name('memorytree')
    12→  .description('MemoryTree — transcript import, dedup, indexing, and session continuity')
    13→  .version('0.1.0')
    14→
    15→// ── init ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    16→
    17→program
    18→  .command('init')
    19→  .description('Initialize a MemoryTree workspace in a repository')
    20→  .option('--root <path>', 'Target repository root', '.')
    21→  .option('--project-name <name>', 'Project name', 'this project')
    22→  .option('--goal-summary <text>', 'Initial goal summary', 'Describe the long-term project goal here.')
    23→  .option('--locale <locale>', 'Template locale: auto, en, or zh-cn', 'auto')
    24→  .option('--date <date>', 'Override date as YYYY-MM-DD')
    25→  .option('--time <time>', 'Override time as HH:MM')
    26→  .option('--skip-agents', 'Deprecated — use upgrade instead')
    27→  .option('--force', 'Overwrite existing generated files')
    28→  .action(async (opts) => {
    29→    const { cmdInit } = await import('./cmd-init.js')
    30→    process.exitCode = cmdInit({
    31→      root: opts.root,
    32→      projectName: opts.projectName,
    33→      goalSummary: opts.goalSummary,
    34→      locale: opts.locale,
    35→      date: opts.date ?? '',
    36→      time: opts.time ?? '',

events.output:
1→# Transcript Archive
     2→
     3→Use this file when the user wants MemoryTree to preserve local AI chat history from Codex, Claude Code, or Gemini CLI.
     4→
     5→## Goal
     6→
     7→Keep a trustworthy transcript record by preserving the current project's transcript mirror and a synchronized global archive at the same time, without mixing unrelated projects into the current repository.
     8→
     9→## Core Rules
    10→
    11→1. Preserve raw transcripts unchanged as the source of truth.
    12→2. Generate cleaned Markdown transcript indexes for discovery, lightweight review, and cross-project search.
    13→3. Keep both the current repository mirror and the user-level global archive updated on every transcript import.
    14→4. Keep the repository mirror scoped to the current project only. When batch discovery sees other projects, archive them in the global archive only.
    15→5. If current-project ownership is ambiguous, treat the transcript as global-only until stronger evidence exists.
    16→6. Ask once per repository whether raw transcript files may also be committed and pushed with MemoryTree.
    17→7. Record that repository-specific answer in `AGENTS.md`.
    18→8. If the answer is not an explicit yes, keep raw transcripts in the repository mirror but exclude them from automatic staging and push.
    19→9. Use deterministic code for transcript cleaning. Do not spend model tokens to normalize transcript content when a parser script can do it.
    20→
    21→## Import Script
    22→
    23→Use `scripts/import-transcripts.py` for one known source file.
    24→
    25→The script must:
    26→
    27→1. Copy the raw transcript into the current repository mirror only when the source belongs to the current repository.
    28→2. Copy the raw transcript into the global archive.
    29→3. Generate the cleaned Markdown transcript in both places with deterministic code when the source matches the current repository, or in the global archive only when it does not.
    30→4. Write manifests and update the global search index in the same operation.
    31→5. Use a concurrency-safe global index, such as SQLite WAL mode, so multiple projects can update the global archive without corrupting it.
    32→6. Refuse snapshot-only or otherwise content-empty sources instead of archiving empty transcript shells.
    33→7. Treat missing or fuzzy project signals as not matched; do not mirror them into the repository on name similarity alone.
    34→
    35→## Discovery Script
    36→
    37→Use `scripts/discover-transcripts.py` when MemoryTree should scan the supported local client stores automatically.
    38→
    39→The script must:
    40→
    41→1. Discover supported transcript files from local Codex, Claude Code, and Gemini stores.
    42→2. Mirror transcripts that belong to the current repository into both the repository and the global archive.
    43→3. Backfill transcripts from unrelated projects into the global archive only.
    44→4. Skip unreadable or malformed transcript files without aborting the full discovery run.
    45→5. Skip snapshot-only or otherwise content-empty files instead of generating empty clean transcripts.
    46→6. Keep deterministic cleaning, manifest generation, and global index updates in the same operation.
    47→7. Support a current-project-only mode when the user wants to avoid global backfill during that run.
    48→8. Keep ambiguous ownership cases out of the repository mirror unless path-level evidence confirms they belong to the current repo.
    49→
    50→## Global Archive Layout
    51→
    52→Recommended user-level layout:
    53→
    54→```text
    55→~/.memorytree/transcripts/
    56→  raw/
    57→    codex/<project>/<yyyy>/<mm>/<session-id>.jsonl
    58→    claude/<project>/<yyyy>/<mm>/<session-id>.jsonl
    59→    gemini/<project>/<yyyy>/<mm>/<session-id>.json
    60→  clean/
    61→    codex/<project>/<yyyy>/<mm>/<session-id>.md
    62→    claude/<project>/<yyyy>/<mm>/<session-id>.md
    63→    gemini/<project>/<yyyy>/<mm>/<session-id>.md
    64→  index/
    65→    sessions.jsonl
    66→    search.sqlite
    67→```
    68→
    69→Path roots:
    70→
    71→- Windows: `%USERPROFILE%`
    72→- macOS and Ubuntu: `$HOME`
    73→
    74→## Project Mirror Layout
    75→
    76→Inside a repository, keep the current project's full mirror:
    77→
    78→```text
    79→Memory/06_transcripts/
    80→  clean/<client>/
    81→  manifests/
    82→  raw/<client>/
    83→```
    84→
    85→Do not place other projects' transcript mirrors inside the current repository.
    86→
    87→For the complete `~/.memorytree/` directory structure, see `references/global-configuration.md`.
    88→
    89→## Client Sources
    90→
    91→### Codex
    92→
    93→- Prefer full session files from `~/.codex/sessions/**/rollout-*.jsonl`.
    94→- Use `~/.codex/session_index.jsonl` for session discovery when helpful.
    95→- Treat `~/.codex/history.jsonl` as lightweight history, not the preferred full transcript source.
    96→
    97→### Claude Code
    98→
    99→- Prefer `transcript_path` when the client exposes it.
   100→- Otherwise prefer `~/.claude/projects/<project>/*.jsonl`.
   101→- Treat `~/.claude/history.jsonl` as lightweight history, not the preferred full transcript source.
   102→- Remember that local transcript retention may be limited by the client's cleanup policy.
   103→
   104→### Gemini CLI
   105→
   106→- Gemini transcript import is supported, but availability depends on saved chats or enabled checkpointing.
   107→- Prefer saved chat or resume-friendly sources first.
   108→- If checkpointing is enabled, use the checkpoint history under `~/.gemini/tmp/<project_hash>/checkpoints` and related Git snapshots under `~/.gemini/history/<project_hash>`.
   109→- If no saved chat or checkpoint source exists, report that no importable Gemini transcript is currently available.
   110→
   111→## Import Rules
   112→
   113→1. Store the raw source unchanged in the global archive, and in the current repository only when the transcript belongs to that repository.
   114→2. Compute and record a content hash for the raw file.
   115→3. Generate a cleaned Markdown file with deterministic code, not model-generated paraphrasing.
   116→4. During batch discovery, mirror only current-project transcripts into the repository. Unrelated projects go to the global archive only.
   117→5. Each cleaned Markdown file must include:
   118→   - client
   119→   - project
   120→   - session id
   121→   - start time
   122→   - cwd or repo path when available
   123→   - branch when available
   124→   - raw source path
   125→   - raw content hash
   126→6. Keep user messages, assistant messages, and useful tool summaries in the cleaned transcript.
   127→7. Drop or fold noisy events such as queue operations, repeated metadata, telemetry, and large low-signal tool dumps.
   128→8. Never treat the cleaned transcript as stronger evidence than the raw transcript.
   129→
   130→## Search Rules
   131→
   132→When the user asks to search all memories or all chat history:
   133→
   134→1. Search `~/.memorytree/transcripts/index/search.sqlite` first for fast full-text lookup.
   135→2. Use `sessions.jsonl` for session-level metadata filtering (client, project, date range).
   136→3. Load the matching cleaned Markdown files for context. When exact wording matters, confirm against the raw transcript.
   137→4. Combine transcript results with the active repository's `Memory/` files when answering.
   138→5. If the search index does not exist, fall back to scanning the `clean/` directory directly.
   139→
   140→## Session Continuity
   141→
   142→When the user opens a new session and asks to see their most recent conversation, the skill provides cross-session context recovery:
   143→
   144→1. Trigger an on-demand sync: immediately scan all three client transcript directories for the current project, bypassing the scheduled heartbeat interval. See `references/heartbeat-scheduling.md`.
   145→2. Query the global index with `project = current directory` and `client IN (claude, codex, gemini)`, sorted by timestamp descending.
   146→3. Exclude the current session: any transcript with `started_at` >= the skill's activation timestamp is considered the current session and excluded. This is cross-client and does not require client-specific session ID knowledge.
   147→4. Load the located transcript: prefer cleaned Markdown for readability, confirm against raw when exact wording matters.
   148→5. Supplement with the latest `Memory/03_chat_logs/` entry if the previous session wrote a chat log summary.
   149→6. Generate a continuation summary (consumes model tokens) that extracts: the problem under discussion, approaches tried, progress made, and what remains unresolved.
   150→7. Include source metadata in the output: client name, session timestamp, approximate duration.
   151→8. If no matching previous session is found for the current project, inform the user: "No previous session found for this project." Do not generate a summary.
   152→
   153→This feature covers all three clients. A user who worked in Codex and then opens Claude Code can recover the Codex session context, and vice versa.
   154→
   155→## Git Rules
   156→
   157→1. Cleaned transcript indexes may be committed as MemoryTree-owned documentation for the current repository.
   158→2. Raw transcript files stay in the current repository mirror regardless of upload approval.
   159→3. Raw transcript files may be staged, committed, and pushed only after the user explicitly approved raw transcript uploads for that repository.
   160→4. Even when raw transcript uploads are approved, keep the PR limited to the current repository's transcript mirror. Do not mix transcript files from unrelated projects.
   161→

1→# Transcript Archive
     2→
     3→Use this file when the user wants MemoryTree to preserve local AI chat history from Codex, Claude Code, or Gemini CLI.
     4→
     5→## Goal
     6→
     7→Keep a trustworthy transcript record by preserving the current project's transcript mirror and a synchronized global archive at the same time, without mixing unrelated projects into the current repository.
     8→
     9→## Core Rules
    10→
    11→1. Preserve raw transcripts unchanged as the source of truth.
    12→2. Generate cleaned Markdown transcript indexes for discovery, lightweight review, and cross-project search.
    13→3. Keep both the current repository mirror and the user-level global archive updated on every transcript import.
    14→4. Keep the repository mirror scoped to the current project only. When batch discovery sees other projects, archive them in the global archive only.
    15→5. If current-project ownership is ambiguous, treat the transcript as global-only until stronger evidence exists.
    16→6. Ask once per repository whether raw transcript files may also be committed and pushed with MemoryTree.
    17→7. Record that repository-specific answer in `AGENTS.md`.
    18→8. If the answer is not an explicit yes, keep raw transcripts in the repository mirror but exclude them from automatic staging and push.
    19→9. Use deterministic code for transcript cleaning. Do not spend model tokens to normalize transcript content when a parser script can do it.
    20→
    21→## Import Script
    22→
    23→Use `scripts/import-transcripts.py` for one known source file.
    24→
    25→The script must:
    26→
    27→1. Copy the raw transcript into the current repository mirror only when the source belongs to the current repository.
    28→2. Copy the raw transcript into the global archive.
    29→3. Generate the cleaned Markdown transcript in both places with deterministic code when the source matches the current repository, or in the global archive only when it does not.
    30→4. Write manifests and update the global search index in the same operation.
    31→5. Use a concurrency-safe global index, such as SQLite WAL mode, so multiple projects can update the global archive without corrupting it.
    32→6. Refuse snapshot-only or otherwise content-empty sources instead of archiving empty transcript shells.
    33→7. Treat missing or fuzzy project signals as not matched; do not mirror them into the repository on name similarity alone.
    34→
    35→## Discovery Script

    44→4. Skip unreadable or malformed transcript files without aborting the full discovery run.
    45→5. Skip snapshot-only or otherwise content-empty files instead of generating empty clean transcripts.
    46→6. Keep deterministic cleaning, manifest generation, and global index updates in the same operation.
    47→7. Support a current-project-only mode when the user wants to avoid global backfill during that run.
    48→8. Keep ambiguous ownership cases out of the repository mirror unless path-level evidence confirms they belong to the current repo.
    49→
    50→## Global Archive Layout

    52→Recommended user-level layout:
    53→

    63→    gemini/<project>/<yyyy>/<mm>/<session-id>.md
    64→  index/
    65→    sessions.jsonl
    66→    search.sqlite
    67→```
    68→
    69→Path roots:
    70→
    71→- Windows: `%USERPROFILE%`
    72→- macOS and Ubuntu: `$HOME`
    73→
    74→## Project Mirror Layout
    75→
    76→Inside a repository, keep the current project's full mirror:
    77→

    84→
    85→Do not place other projects' transcript mirrors inside the current repository.

    88→

events.text:
I now have a complete picture of the entire module. Let me compile the full review. --- # Architecture & Code Quality Review — Report Generation Module **Project:** `memorytree-workflow` at `C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow/` **Reviewer scope:** 21 source + test files, ~1,600 lines of production code, ~700 lines of tests --- ## 1. ARCHITECTURE ### 1.1 Module coupling and single responsibility **MEDIUM — `build.ts` is doing too much** `src/report/build.ts` (393 lines) is the orchestrator, but it also owns three responsibilities that should be in separate modules: - Markdown message parsing (`parseMessagesFromMarkdown`, `parseCleanMarkdownMessages`) — currently exported from the orchestrator, used by tests and by `transcript.ts` indirectly - Raw JSONL tool-extraction (`extractToolSummariesFromRaw`) — a non-trivial parser living inside the build coordinator - `.gitignore` mutation (`ensureGitignore`) — a repo-management utility that belongs either in a `utils/git.ts` file or the existing CLI layer The result is that `build.ts` imports from nearly every other module in the subsystem (8 distinct imports) while also defining core parsing logic. A future developer wanting to understand "how transcripts are parsed" must look inside the orchestrator, not a dedicated parser module. **LOW — `dashboard.ts` exports `clientBadge` for use by sibling renderers** `transcript-list.ts` and `transcript.ts` both import `clientBadge` from `dashboard.ts`. This creates a coupling between sibling render modules through what should be a shared utility. `clientBadge` belongs in `layout.ts` or a small `render/utils.ts` alongside `escHtml` and `renderNav`, not in a page-level renderer. **LOW — Nav path encoding is fragile** `layout.ts` has two hardcoded nav arrays (`NAV_ITEMS` and `NAV_ITEMS_ROOT`) differentiated by a `rootLevel: boolean` flag. The `../` prefix on all non-root hrefs assumes a single level of nesting. Transcripts are at `transcripts/{client}/{stem}.html` — two levels deep — but `renderNav` is called with `rootLevel = false` which produces one-level-up hrefs. This actually works only because the nav bar links to index pages one level up, but the assumption is invisible and breaks if the nesting depth ever increases or if a page is served from a deeper path. --- ## 2. CODE QUALITY ### 2.1 File sizes All files are well within limits. The largest production file is `build.ts` at 393 lines, `charts.ts` at 315 lines, and `css.ts` at 346 lines (primarily a string literal). All others are under 175 lines. No file approaches the 800-line ceiling. ### 2.2 Function lengths All functions are under 50 lines with two exceptions: **LOW — `renderHeatmap` in `charts.ts` is 68 lines (lines 24–92)** Still readable, but could be split: date-range generation and cell-rect generation are independent concerns. **LOW — `parseMessagesFromMarkdown` in `build.ts` is 55 lines (lines 207–261)** Just over the 50-line guideline. Not a meaningful problem, but worth noting. ### 2.3 Magic numbers and hardcoded values **LOW — Hardcoded pixel constants in `charts.ts`** `renderHeatmap` and `renderClientDoughnut` contain magic layout numbers (`cellSize = 11`, `cx = 90`, `r = 60`, `innerR = 38`, etc.) with no named constants. These are grouped coherently but are not self-documenting. **LOW — Hardcoded color strings duplicated between `css.ts` and `charts.ts`** `HEATMAP_COLORS` in `charts.ts` duplicates the `--green-*` CSS variable values. If the theme colors change, both files must be updated. SVG cannot use CSS variables directly, so this is a known trade-off, but it should at minimum be documented. --- ## 3. CONSISTENCY ### 3.1 Immutability The codebase follows the immutability guideline well overall. **HIGH — `links.ts` mutates arrays in-place (lines 50–64)** ```ts const fwd = forwardLinks[m.session_id] if (fwd) { if (!fwd.includes(targetId)) fwd.push(targetId) // mutation } else { forwardLinks[m.session_id] = [targetId] } ``` The arrays stored in `forwardLinks` and `backlinks` are mutated with `.push()`. The project coding standard is "NEVER mutate existing ones — return new copies." The immutable pattern here would be either `forwardLinks[id] = [...existing, targetId]` or using a `Map>` to deduplicate. This is the most obvious consistency violation in the entire module. **MEDIUM — Module-level mutable state in `summarize.ts` (line 30)** ```ts let activeCalls = 0 ``` This is a module-level integer incremented and decremented by concurrent promises. It works correctly as written (single Node.js process, event loop is not preemptive), but it is a hidden side effect. A semaphore implemented as a class or a pure-functional token bucket would be safer and more testable. The current pattern is also not reset between tests — if the test runner ever runs `getSummary` in a mode where the API is reachable, `activeCalls` could leak across tests. ### 3.2 Error handling Error handling is consistent and conservative throughout: all I/O is wrapped in try/catch and failures are silently skipped to avoid aborting the build. This matches the stated goal of resilience. The only concern is: **LOW — Suppressed errors lose diagnostic information in `build.ts` (lines 76–82)** The catch block around tool extraction is completely silent. Other modules at least use `console.warn`. A single `console.warn('[build] tool extraction failed:', rawPath)` would help diagnose data gaps without breaking the build. --- ## 4. TEST COVERAGE ### 4.1 Overall assessment Coverage of the pure-logic layer (`stats.ts`, `summarize.ts`, `links.ts`, `search.ts`, `charts.ts`) is comprehensive and well-structured. The integration test in `build.test.ts` covers the happy path thoroughly. ### 4.2 Missing edge cases **HIGH — No test for XSS injection through manifest data** `renderTranscriptList`, `renderDashboard`, and `renderTranscript` all embed `m.title`, `m.client`, `m.session_id`, `m.cwd`, and `m.branch` into HTML via `escHtml`. There is no test verifying that a manifest with `title: ''` produces escaped output. Given that manifests are parsed from JSON files that could be tampered with, this is a meaningful security boundary that should be tested. **HIGH — No test for the inline JS injection path in `renderSearchPage`** `renderSearchPage` embeds `JSON.stringify(index)` directly into a `` in the title or snippet does not break out of the script context. For example, a title of `foo` would produce a broken/malicious page. `JSON.stringify` does NOT escape `` sequences. This is a real XSS vector. **MEDIUM — No test for `renderMessageBody` / `applyInlineMarkdown` in `transcript.ts`** The inline markdown renderer is not tested at all. Edge cases that could produce incorrect HTML include: nested emphasis (`**bold *and italic* bold**`), backtick-in-bold, and the handling of single `\n` vs. `\n\n` (the current implementation only creates paragraphs on `\n\n`, single newlines become `
` inside `applyInlineMarkdown` but are treated as paragraph content in `renderInlineText`). There is also no test for the code-block regex behaviour with unterminated backticks. **MEDIUM — No test for `markdownToHtml` in `markdown.ts`** The custom markdown converter handles headings, lists, code blocks, YAML front-matter, and inline formatting. There are no unit tests for any of these paths. Edge cases: YAML front-matter only skipped at `i === 0`, so a second `---` block would not be treated as front matter; nested lists are not supported (the `olMatch`/`ulMatch` regexes ignore indentation); the horizontal-rule handler fires for `---` which conflicts with YAML closing delimiter detection. **MEDIUM — `buildReport` test does not verify transcript HTML content** The integration test (`build.test.ts` line 189) only asserts that an HTML file exists. It does not assert that the transcript HTML contains the expected message text, the session title, or that `escHtml` was applied. A regression in rendering would not be caught. **LOW — `buildDayBuckets` / `buildWeekBuckets` do not test the ISO week boundary correctness** `isoWeek` in `stats.ts` implements an ISO-8601 week calculation (Thursday-anchor). The test for `buildWeekBuckets` only checks the format regex (`/^\d{4}-W\d{2}$/`) and the total count. It does not verify the actual week number for a known date, so an off-by-one in the calculation would pass. **LOW — `buildSearchIndex` budget logic is tested loosely** The test verifies that `json.length <= 51_000` with 100 sessions each having 200-char snippets. This passes comfortably, but the boundary case — where a single entry is so large that `remaining < 20` truncates to nothing — is not tested. --- ## 5. DEAD CODE / REDUNDANCY **HIGH — `transcriptHref` is duplicated in three files** The function that computes a relative URL from a `ManifestEntry` to its HTML file is independently implemented in: - `dashboard.ts` line 132–136 - `transcript-list.ts` line 68–72 - `transcript.ts` line 161–166 The three versions are slightly different (root-relative vs. one-level-up vs. two-levels-up from the caller's location). This is the most significant maintenance hazard in the module — a future change to the output path structure would require finding and updating all three. This logic belongs in a single exported function in `layout.ts` that accepts the caller's depth as a parameter, or in a dedicated `render/utils.ts`. **MEDIUM — `GoalFile` and `KnowledgeFile` interfaces are identical** Both `goals.ts` and `knowledge.ts` define: ```ts export interface { filename: string; title: string; content: string } ``` These are separate named types, which is fine conceptually, but they are structurally identical and both modules implement an identical `slugify` function. A shared `MarkdownFile` interface and a shared `slugify` utility would eliminate the duplication. **MEDIUM — `build.ts` `loadMarkdownFiles` duplicates what `extractMarkdownTitle` already does in a slightly different location** `loadMarkdownFiles` (line 336) and `extractMarkdownTitle` (line 362) are private helpers in `build.ts` that serve knowledge/goals page generation. This logic is entirely unrelated to the build orchestration concern of `build.ts` and should not be there at all — it should be co-located with `goals.ts` and `knowledge.ts`, or in a shared util. **LOW — `pdf: false` is passed to `buildReport` by `cmd-report.ts` but `BuildReportOptions.pdf` is never read** `BuildReportOptions` has a `pdf?: boolean` field defined in `types/report.ts`. `buildReport` in `build.ts` accepts it in the options type but never reads it. `cmd-report.ts` always passes `pdf: false`. The `pdf` flag appears to be a placeholder for a feature not yet implemented, but it adds noise to the public interface and could mislead callers into expecting PDF output. --- ## 6. PERFORMANCE **MEDIUM — Unbounded `Promise.all` parallelism with a polling semaphore** `build.ts` launches one promise per manifest (line 70–105) and calls `Promise.all` on all of them. The concurrency limit of 5 is enforced in `summarize.ts` via busy-polling (`setTimeout(resolve, 100)` in a while loop). This means: 1. All file I/O (reading clean markdown, resolving raw paths, writing HTML) runs unbounded in parallel. For a repository with thousands of transcripts, this will exhaust Node.js file descriptor limits and produce contention. 2. The 100ms polling interval introduces latency proportional to concurrency contention — if all 5 slots are busy, new tasks wait up to 100ms to start. A proper solution would use a streaming processor (batches of N at a time) that gates both I/O and AI calls behind the semaphore, rather than scattering the gate only around the AI call. **LOW — `manifests.find()` inside a `.map()` in `build.ts` (line 96–99)** ```ts const backlinkManifests = backlinkIds .map(id => manifests.find(x => x.session_id === id)) ``` This is O(N × M) where N = backlink count and M = total manifests. For large datasets this should be a pre-built lookup map (`Map`). The same pattern appears to be intended in `links.ts` (where `lookup` is already a plain object), but `build.ts` does not share or reuse it. **LOW — `buildSearchIndex` calls `JSON.stringify(entry)` twice per entry (lines 33, 41)** The size check serializes the entry to measure bytes, then serializes it again after the potential truncation. One of these could be eliminated. --- ## 7. MAINTAINABILITY **MEDIUM — Inline JavaScript embedded in a TypeScript template string (`search.ts` lines 85–138)** The vanilla JS search logic is a 50-line string inside `renderSearchPage`. It has its own `escHtml` function that duplicates the one in `layout.ts`. This code is not type-checked, not linted, and not directly testable in isolation. The tests for it only verify substring presence. A change to the search logic requires editing a template string — no autocomplete, no type errors. This should be extracted to a static `.js` asset file bundled/inlined at build time, or at minimum the function-level duplication (`escHtml`) should be eliminated. **LOW — `isoWeek` implementation is duplicated between `stats.ts` and `charts.ts`** `isoWeek` (in `stats.ts`, line 134) and `toIsoWeek` (in `charts.ts`, line 263) implement the same ISO week calculation with identical logic. They are separate private functions. The canonical version should be exported from `stats.ts` and imported by `charts.ts`. **LOW — No type guard on parsed manifest data** `loadManifestsRecursive` in `build.ts` performs a type assertion (`as ManifestEntry`) after checking only `session_id` and `client`. A manifest missing `message_count` or `started_at` would pass the guard and cause silent arithmetic errors downstream (NaN in stats, empty date ranges). A schema validation step (even a manual field check) would catch malformed manifests early. --- ## 8. PLAN COMPLIANCE **CRITICAL — Cache location deviates from spec** The plan spec (and the `references/transcript-archive.md` global configuration document) places the global search index and derived artifacts at `~/.memorytree/`. The cache for AI summaries is placed at `join(output, '.cache')` — inside `Memory/07_reports/.cache/`. This means: - The cache is **inside the output directory**, which is gitignored as `Memory/07_reports/`. The cache is therefore gitignored and lost every time the output directory is cleaned. - If the user specifies a custom `--output`, the cache follows it rather than being stable across output changes. The spec implies the cache should be stable and persistent, decoupled from the ephemeral output. It should live at a path like `join(root, 'Memory', '07_reports', '.cache')` that is stable regardless of the `--output` flag, or at a user-level path like `~/.memorytree/report-cache/`. As currently written, re-running `report build` after `rm -rf Memory/07_reports` will re-issue API calls for every transcript. **HIGH — Model ID used as default does not match the plan's stated model** `build.ts` line 32 and `main.ts` line 177 both reference `claude-haiku-4-5-20251001`. The performance guidelines specify `claude-haiku-4-5` as the appropriate lightweight model for worker agents. The date-stamped model ID `20251001` is an internal version that may be deprecated. The CLI `--model` default in `main.ts` and the `DEFAULT_MODEL` constant in `build.ts` must be kept in sync manually — if one changes, the other becomes stale. These should reference a single constant defined once. **LOW — `pdf?: boolean` in `BuildReportOptions` is exposed in the type but never implemented** If the plan included a PDF output feature, it is not present. If it was descoped, the option should be removed from the interface to avoid confusion. --- ## Summary Table | # | Severity | File(s) | Issue | |---|----------|---------|-------| | 1 | CRITICAL | `build.ts` | Cache placed inside gitignored output dir;
